title: Boat Rental in Samoa: Guide to Costs, Seasons and Vessels for 2026
html:
Chartering a vessel in Samoa offers direct access to the South Pacific’s most pristine reefs, with trips from Apia Harbour starting at around €250 for a half-day on a small motorboat. The islands of Upolu and Savai‘i set the stage for unforgettable itineraries, and BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners across the archipelago.

Where to set off in Samoa

Most boat rentals depart from the north coast of Upolu or the sheltered bays of Savai‘i. Apia Harbour is the main commercial and charter port, while Mulifanua Wharf serves ferries and private launches. On Savai‘i, Salelologa Harbour provides access to the island’s southern reefs. For secluded anchorages, head to Namua Island off the east coast or the calm waters of Aleipata Bay, both popular with overnight charters.

Which boat to rent

Selecting the right vessel depends on your group size and itinerary. Below are the recommended options for Samoa waters.

  1. Motorboat (6, 8 m): Best for coastal fishing and reef snorkelling; carries up to 6 guests; from €250 half-day.
  2. Catamaran (12, 15 m): Stable and spacious for family day trips; up to 12 guests; from €800 full-day.
  3. Sailing yacht (10, 14 m): Ideal for multi-day exploring of the outer islands; sleeps 4, 6; from €1,200 per day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (e.g., Princess V58): Luxury option for special occasions; up to 10 guests; from €2,500 full-day. For specifications, see the Princess V58 listing on BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Samoa?

Daily charter rates in Samoa range from €250 for a small motorboat to over €3,000 for a fully crewed yacht. Costs depend on vessel size, duration and whether fuel and crew are included. A good reference is yacht charter Samoa options on BoatHire24, where you can compare verified boat listings.

  • Shared tour (snorkelling excursion): from €80 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 6 guests): from €250.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): from €800.
  • Crewed yacht full-day (up to 10 guests): from €2,000, typically including skipper and lunch.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Samoa?

No local licence is required if you charter a crewed boat, but bareboat hire may require a recognised certificate or a short sea trial. The operator will confirm exact requirements during booking.

Request a briefing on Samoan reef etiquette, especially the no-anchoring zones near Aleipata islands, where mooring buoys are provided by local marine reserves.

When is the best season for boating in Samoa?

The ideal season runs from May to October when the southeast trade winds keep seas calm and visibility high. These months offer the driest weather and steady breezes ideal for sailing. From November to April, the wet season brings heavier rain and occasional cyclones, though short passages remain possible in sheltered bays.

At a glance

  • Price band: €250, €3,000 per day.
  • Peak months: May, October.
  • Trip length: 4, 8 hours typical; multi-day available.
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests usually.
  • Currency: EUR (quoted; local payment may be in WST).

Questions about boat rental in Samoa

Can I rent a boat in Samoa without a skipper?

Bareboat rentals are limited to experienced skippers who can demonstrate competence, but most charter companies require you to hire a local skipper for safety and local knowledge.

What is the best island to visit by boat in Samoa?

Upolu’s south coast offers the most sheltered anchorages and coral gardens, while Savai‘i’s Alofaaga Blowholes are a spectacular excursion for a full-day charter.

Are there any hidden costs in Samoa boat rental?

Fuel is sometimes quoted separately, and a mandatory cleaning fee of around €50 may apply, so always confirm what is included before booking.

How far is it from Apia to the nearest snorkelling spots?

Palolo Deep Marine Reserve is only 2 nautical miles from Apia Harbour, reachable in under 20 minutes on a fast motorboat.

Whether you plan a day excursion to Namua or a week-long voyage around Savai‘i, BoatHire24 lets you compare vessels and itineraries across the South Pacific. If you own a boat in Samoa, you can list your boat on the platform to reach thousands of travellers.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Samoa and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
